name: PRIMITIVE NEUROECTODERMAL TUMOR OF SPINAL CORD

demographics.Location: spinal cord, intradural-extramedullary compartment, extradural compartment
demographics.Age: more common in adults than children; M÷F = 6÷4
demographics.Histo: small round blue cells with hyperchromatic nuclei + scanty cytoplasm, frequent mitoses

distribution: spinal cord, intradural-extramedullary compartment, extradural compartment

clinical.Spread: throughout CSF space into cranium, lung, bone, lymph node
